# Open Customs Toolbox

> A faithful, fully-sourced PostgreSQL reference data model of the UNCTAD ASYCUDA World (SYDONIA) customs management system, reconstructed from public documentation only. 55 tables across 8 modules (manifest, SAD declaration, valuation, taxes, selectivity, accounting, transit/suspense, audit), for sandbox, analytics, integration and training use.

Every `CREATE TABLE` is grounded in a cited public source (`-- src:`) or honestly marked `-- inferred` (49 documented / 6 inferred). The model loads into a dedicated `asycuda` PostgreSQL schema and ships with reference seed data and a worked end-to-end example (manifest → declaration → valuation → taxes → selectivity → payment → release). This is a reference reconstruction — it contains no proprietary ASYCUDA schema, pirated software, or data from any live customs system.

For the entire documentation as a single file, see [llms-full.txt](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/llms-full.txt).

## Getting started

- [Quickstart](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/getting-started/quickstart/): create a database and run the end-to-end example in one minute.
- [Installation](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/getting-started/installation/): PostgreSQL 14+, the `asycuda` schema, idempotent reloads, Docker, troubleshooting.
- [Customs concepts](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/getting-started/concepts/): domain primer — manifest, bill of lading, the SAD, valuation, taxes, selectivity, lifecycle — mapped to tables.

## Schema

- [Schema overview](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/schema/): the 8 modules, conventions, and provenance legend.
- [Reference & configuration](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/schema/reference-config/): the `ref_*` code tables (ISO/UN/WCO standards) plus traders and system users.
- [Manifest & cargo](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/schema/manifest/): manifest, bills of lading (master/house degroupage), containers, cargo lines.
- [Declaration (the SAD)](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/schema/declaration/): general + item segments, valuation notes, tax lines, attached/previous documents.
- [Selectivity & risk](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/schema/selectivity/): risk criteria, GREEN/YELLOW/RED/BLUE lanes, inspection acts.
- [Accounting](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/schema/accounting/): accounts, payments, receipts, ledger movements, guarantees.
- [Transit & suspense](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/schema/transit-suspense/): warehousing, transit, temporary admission.
- [Audit & workflow](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/schema/audit/): the audit log and status-history pattern.
- [Entity-relationship diagram](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/schema/erd/): every foreign key, rendered from the loaded schema.
- [Data dictionary](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/schema/data-dictionary/): every table and column with type, nullability and source.

## The platform (ASYCUDA itself)

- [The platform](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/platform/): which ASYCUDA version this toolbox models (ASYCUDA World, v4) and how to fingerprint the one you have.
- [Version lineage](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/platform/versions/): the five generations (v1 1981 → v2 → ASYCUDA++ → ASYCUDA World → ASY5/New Generation), compared.
- [ASYCUDA World](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/platform/asycuda-world/): the modeled version in depth — XML e-document model, Java client, closed protocol, and how our schema relates to the unpublished physical one.
- [XML messages & wire format](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/platform/xml-messages/): the SAD Box → AW XML tag → toolbox column map, the `<ASYCUDA>`-root wire-format gotcha, the AWMDS manifest stream, WCO Data Model and EDIFACT lineage.
- [Integration surfaces](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/platform/integration/): the doors that actually exist (RDBMS/ETL, ASYHUB, IATA Cargo-XML, XML import, ASY5) and the restricted specs you must request.
- [Selectivity & clearance](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/platform/selectivity-clearance/): the clearance state machine (C/L/PRN serials) and four-lane risk model, mapped to the schema.
- [Further reading](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/platform/resources/): curated map of the public ASYCUDA documentation landscape (~90 sources) and what is member-restricted.

## Guides

- [Loading the schema](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/guides/loading/): integration patterns — CI, test resets, coexisting with your own tables, namespacing.
- [Querying the model](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/guides/querying/): the key join paths and an analytics cookbook (revenue by HS, assessed-vs-paid, lane throughput, warehouse stock).
- [Extending the schema](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/guides/extending/): add tables/columns while keeping conventions and provenance intact.
- [Worked example](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/guides/worked-example/): `e2e.sql` narrated step by step.
- [ML on customs data](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/guides/ml-risk-engine/): the ML risk-engine blueprint — DATE/BACUDA features mapped to SAD boxes and toolbox columns, labels from the Inspection Act, the lane loop, prototyping on this schema.

## Provenance

- [Reconstruction methodology](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/provenance/methodology/): the evidence-first loop, source policy, and verification gates.
- [Sources](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/provenance/sources/): the 20 cited public documents with URLs and notes.
- [Coverage](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/provenance/coverage/): every module/table marked documented / partial / inferred, with known gaps.
- [Official fit & gap](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/provenance/fit/): table-by-table mapping against the official UNCTAD/DTL ASYCUDA World tables.

## Claude Code skills

- [Claude Code skills](https://francoischastel.github.io/OpenCustomsToolbox/skills/): a suite of skills (setup, query, seed, extend, validate) to use the model on your own codebase.

## Optional

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/FrancoisChastel/OpenCustomsToolbox): the SQL schema, seed data, worked example, cached sources, and skills.
- [Schema SQL](https://github.com/FrancoisChastel/OpenCustomsToolbox/blob/main/Sydonia/schema/asycuda.sql): the 55-table DDL with inline provenance tags.
